title: Customer Identification Program
description: Verify and document customer identity at account opening per BSA CIP requirements with risk-based procedures.

title: Beneficial Ownership Identification
description: Identify and verify beneficial owners holding 25% or more equity for legal entity customers per FinCEN CDD Rule.

title: Suspicious Activity Report Filing
description: File SAR within 30 days of detection for suspicious transactions of $5,000 or more involving funds derived from illegal activity.

title: Currency Transaction Report
description: File CTR for currency transactions exceeding $10,000 in a single business day per BSA reporting requirements.

title: OFAC Sanctions Screening
description: Screen all customers and transactions against OFAC SDN list before processing, with daily list refresh.

title: Enhanced Due Diligence for High-Risk Customers
description: Apply EDD procedures for PEPs, foreign correspondent accounts, and high-risk geographies per BSA risk-based guidance.

title: AML Program Designation
description: Designate qualified BSA officer and document AML compliance program with board approval.

title: AML Independent Testing
description: Conduct independent testing of AML program at least annually by qualified internal audit or external auditor.

title: AML Training Program
description: Provide ongoing AML training to all relevant personnel with documented completion tracking.

title: Record Retention - AML
description: Retain AML records including CIP, transaction monitoring, and SAR documentation for at least five years.

title: Funds Travel Rule
description: Include originator and beneficiary information for funds transfers of $3,000 or more per Travel Rule.

title: Section 314(a) Information Sharing
description: Respond to FinCEN 314(a) information requests within 14 days with documented search procedures.

title: Foreign Bank Certification
description: Obtain certification regarding shell banks and foreign correspondent accounts annually.

title: Marijuana Banking Reporting
description: Apply BSA expectations to marijuana-related businesses with limited, priority, or termination SAR designation.

title: Risk-Based AML Approach
description: Implement risk-based AML controls aligned with FFIEC BSA/AML examination manual.

title: Cardholder Data Encryption at Rest
description: Render PAN unreadable using strong cryptography wherever stored; truncated/tokenized PAN preferred.

title: Strong Cryptography in Transit
description: Use TLS 1.2 minimum (TLS 1.3 preferred) for all cardholder data transmissions over open networks.

title: Need-to-Know Access
description: Limit access to cardholder data by business need-to-know with documented role-based access controls.

title: Multi-Factor Authentication
description: Implement MFA for all non-console administrative access and remote access to CDE.

title: Audit Trail for Cardholder Data Access
description: Log all access to cardholder data with user identification, event type, timestamp, and outcome.

title: One Year of Log Retention
description: Retain audit logs for at least one year with three months immediately available for analysis.

title: Quarterly Vulnerability Scans
description: Conduct internal and external network vulnerability scans at least quarterly and after any significant change.

title: Annual Penetration Testing
description: Perform external and internal penetration testing at least annually and after significant infrastructure changes.

title: Secure Coding Practices
description: Develop software using secure coding practices including OWASP Top 10 protections.

title: Code Review Before Production
description: Review custom code prior to release to production for security vulnerabilities.

title: Token Vault Segregation
description: Segregate token vault from CDE with strict access controls when using tokenization.

title: Cryptographic Key Lifecycle
description: Manage cryptographic keys with documented generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction procedures.

title: CDE Network Segmentation
description: Implement network segmentation to isolate CDE from other systems with documented controls.

title: Incident Response Plan
description: Maintain documented incident response plan with cardholder data breach notification procedures.

title: Service Provider Due Diligence
description: Maintain documented due diligence for service providers handling cardholder data.

title: Lawful Basis for Processing
description: Document lawful basis (consent, contract, legitimate interest) before processing personal data.

title: Right of Access
description: Respond to data subject access requests within one month with comprehensive personal data export.

title: Right to Erasure
description: Implement right-to-be-forgotten with verifiable deletion across all systems and backups.

title: Right to Data Portability
description: Provide personal data in structured, commonly used, machine-readable format on request.

title: Granular Consent
description: Obtain freely given, specific, informed consent with separate granular opt-ins per processing purpose.

title: 72-Hour Breach Notification
description: Notify supervisory authority of personal data breach within 72 hours of awareness.

title: Data Subject Breach Notification
description: Notify affected data subjects without undue delay when breach poses high risk.

title: Data Protection by Design
description: Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures by design and by default.

title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
description: Conduct DPIA for high-risk processing including profiling and large-scale special category processing.

title: DPO Appointment
description: Appoint Data Protection Officer where core activities involve large-scale personal data monitoring.

title: Cross-Border Transfer Safeguards
description: Use SCCs, BCRs, or adequacy decisions for personal data transfers outside the EEA.

title: Records of Processing Activities
description: Maintain Article 30 records of processing activities for all controllers and processors.

title: Security Trust Services Criteria
description: Implement controls aligned with AICPA TSC Common Criteria for security.

title: Availability Trust Services Criteria
description: Implement availability controls including monitoring, capacity planning, and disaster recovery.

title: Processing Integrity Controls
description: Implement processing integrity controls ensuring complete, valid, accurate, timely, and authorized processing.

title: Confidentiality Controls
description: Identify, classify, and protect confidential information per AICPA TSC C1 criteria.

title: Continuous Monitoring
description: Implement continuous monitoring of system performance and security events with automated alerting.

title: Vendor Risk Management
description: Assess and monitor vendors with access to systems or data per AICPA TSC CC9.

title: Change Management Process
description: Document and test changes to systems with approval workflow before production deployment.

title: Security Incident Response
description: Maintain incident response procedures with detection, escalation, containment, and post-incident review.

title: Logical Access Controls
description: Implement role-based access with periodic review, removal upon termination, and least-privilege defaults.

title: Physical Access Controls
description: Restrict and log physical access to facilities and equipment supporting in-scope systems.

title: ISMS Establishment
description: Establish, implement, maintain, and improve an Information Security Management System.

title: Information Security Risk Assessment
description: Conduct documented information security risk assessments at planned intervals.

title: Asset Inventory and Classification
description: Maintain inventory of information assets with classification and ownership assignment.

title: Cryptographic Key Management Policy
description: Define and implement policy on cryptographic controls including key lifecycle management.

title: Supplier Information Security
description: Address information security in supplier agreements and monitor supplier service delivery.

title: Business Continuity Management
description: Plan, implement, verify, and review information security continuity during adverse situations.

title: Secure System Engineering Principles
description: Apply secure system engineering principles for design, implementation, and operation.

title: Internal ISMS Audit
description: Conduct internal audits of ISMS at planned intervals per ISO 27001 clause 9.2.

title: Order Audit Trail (OATS)
description: Capture and report all order events to FINRA OATS within timeframes per Rule 7440 series.

title: Books and Records Retention
description: Preserve books and records per SEC Rule 17a-4 in non-rewriteable, non-erasable format for retention period.

title: Communications with Public
description: Pre-approve retail communications by registered principal and file with FINRA Advertising Regulation Department.

title: Supervisory System
description: Establish and maintain a supervisory system reasonably designed to achieve compliance with applicable rules.

title: Best Execution
description: Use reasonable diligence to ascertain best market for customer transactions and obtain best execution.

title: Anti-Money Laundering Compliance
description: Implement AML compliance program meeting FINRA Rule 3310 and BSA requirements.

title: Associated Person Registration
description: Register associated persons with FINRA and ensure required qualification examinations are passed.

title: Outside Business Activities
description: Require written notice and supervisory approval of outside business activities by associated persons.

title: Know Your Customer
description: Use reasonable diligence to know essential facts about customer accounts under FINRA Rule 2090.

title: Suitability Obligation
description: Have reasonable basis to believe recommendations are suitable for customer per FINRA Rule 2111.

title: MiFID II Transaction Reporting
description: Report transactions to NCA T+1 with all 65 RTS 22 data fields including LEI identification.

title: MiFID II Best Execution
description: Take all sufficient steps to obtain best possible result for clients with annual RTS 27/28 reports.

title: Product Governance Requirements
description: Maintain product governance arrangements identifying target market and ensuring distribution alignment.

title: Costs and Charges Disclosure
description: Provide ex-ante and ex-post disclosure of all costs and charges including aggregated and itemized views.

title: Research Payment Unbundling
description: Separate execution costs from research payments via RPA or P&L absorption per MiFID II.

title: Internal Control over Financial Reporting
description: Establish and maintain ICFR with annual management assessment per SOX Section 404(a).

title: CEO/CFO Certification
description: Provide quarterly and annual SEC filings certifications by CEO and CFO per SOX Section 302.

title: Audit Committee Independence
description: Maintain audit committee composed of independent directors per SOX Section 301.

title: Whistleblower Protection
description: Provide anonymous reporting channels and prohibit retaliation against whistleblowers.

title: Documentation Retention
description: Retain audit working papers for at least seven years per SOX Section 802.

title: Truth in Lending Disclosure
description: Provide TILA disclosures including APR, finance charge, amount financed, and total of payments.

title: Right of Rescission
description: Provide three-business-day rescission period for non-purchase-money mortgage credit transactions.

title: Electronic Fund Transfer Disclosures
description: Provide EFT disclosures including liability limits and dispute procedures at account opening.

title: Error Resolution Procedures
description: Investigate and resolve EFT errors within 10 business days with provisional credit if needed.

title: Adverse Action Notice
description: Provide adverse action notice when credit application is denied based on credit report.

title: FCRA Permissible Purpose
description: Obtain consumer reports only for FCRA permissible purposes with documented evidence.

title: Fair Debt Collection Practices
description: Conduct debt collection activities per FDCPA prohibitions on harassment and false statements.

title: GLBA Privacy Notice
description: Provide initial and annual privacy notices to consumers describing information sharing practices.

title: GLBA Safeguards Rule
description: Develop, implement, and maintain comprehensive information security program for customer data.

title: Common Equity Tier 1 Ratio
description: Maintain CET1 capital ratio of at least 4.5 percent of risk-weighted assets.

title: Liquidity Coverage Ratio
description: Maintain LCR of at least 100 percent ensuring sufficient HQLA for 30-day stress period.

title: Third-Party Risk Management
description: Implement risk-based third-party risk management program per FFIEC outsourcing technology services guidance.

title: Cybersecurity Assessment Tool
description: Use FFIEC CAT to assess inherent risk and cybersecurity maturity at the institution level.

title: Funds Availability Schedule
description: Provide check funds availability per Reg CC schedule with disclosures and notices.

title: Truth in Savings Disclosure
description: Provide APY, fees, and terms disclosures for deposit accounts per Truth in Savings Act.

title: Strong Customer Authentication
description: Apply SCA for electronic payments with two of three factors (knowledge, possession, inherence).

title: Open Banking API Standards
description: Provide PSD2-compliant APIs to TPPs with consent management and dispute resolution procedures.

title: FATCA Reporting
description: Identify US persons and report account information annually to IRS or local tax authority.

title: Common Reporting Standard
description: Identify and report financial accounts of foreign tax residents per OECD CRS.

title: California Consumer Privacy Rights
description: Honor CCPA rights including right to know, delete, opt out of sale, and non-discrimination.
